model.label: Claude Sonnet 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Fast flagship Claude model for coding, analysis, and high-throughput agent use.

pick.rationale: Miami (FL) holds a consistent edge in every major efficiency metric, ranking #30 by KenPom (AdjEM +20.67) and #35 by Torvik versus Missouri's #52 and #46 respectively, with the Hurricanes' defense (AdjDE 100.7, 45th nationally) presenting a meaningful matchup problem for a Tigers offense ranked 50th nationally. Miami's 25-8 ACC record reflects genuine schedule-strength battle-testing, and their two-way efficiency profile — top-35 nationally on both ends — is a level Missouri's 20-12 SEC squad, with a defense ranked 68th in AdjDE, cannot match. No significant injury concerns surfaced for either team, and while 10-over-7 upsets occur at a historically meaningful rate (~35%), Missouri's defensive deficiencies and the consistent efficiency gap across all major rating systems make Miami the right side in this matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Miami (FL) vs. Missouri — West Region 7/10 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Miami holds a consistent ~5-point AdjEM advantage over Missouri across both KenPom and Torvik, with a significantly better defense (AdjDE 100.7 vs. 104.1) and a stronger overall résumé from ACC play. Missouri is a live 10-seed underdog with SEC credentials, but their defensive profile and inferior efficiency ratings tip this to Miami.
evidence: Miami KenPom #30, AdjEM +20.67, AdjOE 121.4 (34th), AdjDE 100.7 (45th); Torvik #35
Missouri KenPom #52, AdjEM +15.36, AdjOE 119.5 (50th), AdjDE 104.1 (68th); Torvik #46
Miami 25-8 record in ACC; Missouri 20-12 in SEC
Miami lost to Virginia 84-62 in ACC Tournament semis; Missouri lost to Kentucky 78-72 in SEC Tournament
No significant injury concerns identified for either team heading into the tournament
Miami is 7-seed, Missouri is 10-seed; 10-over-7 upsets occur ~35% historically
Betting market seeding consensus aligns with Miami as moderate favorite

result.text: Miami March Madness opponent is Missouri in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Miami basketball’s March Madness opponent is Missouri. See the bracket Dan Rorabaugh USA TODAY NETWORK - Florida Show Caption Hide Caption Who’s ready to put on the glass slipper this March? Two underdog teams that could make a Cinderella run this March Madness are Saint Louis and Miami (Ohio). (This story has been updated to add new information about the other teams in Miami's March Madness bracket.) The Miami men's basketball team will play Missouri in the first round of the 2026 NCAA tournament . The Hurricanes (25-8) were awarded the No. 7 seed in the West Region. Jai Lucas ' first March Madness opponent as head coach will be the Tigers (20-12) out of the SEC. Miami completed a stunning turnaround in 2026 after going 7-24 last season. They returned to the postseason behind first-team All-ACC forward Malik Reneau and second-team guard Tre Donaldson, both Florida natives who transferred in from Indiana and Michigan, respectively. UM finished third in the ACC regular-season standings and reached the semifinals of the ACC tournament . Buy Miami NCAA tournament tickets The Hurricanes' biggest win of the season came Feb. 10 against North Carolina, when they beat Caleb Wilson and the Tar Heels 75-66 at the Watsco Center. They last appeared in the NCAA tournament in 2023, when they reached the Final Four as a 5-seed before losing to eventful champion UConn. Who does Miami play in 2026 NCAA tournament? Miami faces the Missouri Tigers in the first round. Mizzou went 20-12 and finished seventh in the SEC this season, though they were bounced by Kentucky in their SEC tournament opener in the quarterfinals. Missouri is in the big dance for the third time in four years under coach Dennis Gates. The Tigers boasted the No. 5 defense in their conference at 75.3 points per game and were 12th in offense at 79.7 ppg, though they were second in shooting 49% from the floor. Their best player is Mark Mitchell, a 6-foot-8 senior forward who made the All-SEC second team. He led the team in scoring with 18.3 ppg, good for sixth in the SEC, and in rebounding with 5.3 boards per game. Mizzou has two other players averaging double-digit points, Australian guard Jayden Stone and forward Trent Pierce. Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today! result.title: Virginia Rolls Past Miami 84-62 to Advance to ACC Tournament Title Game
result.snippet: Virginia improved to 3-1 all-time in the ACC Tournament against Miami and 49-67 overall in ACC Tournament play. Virginia's 22-point win is tied for the fifth-largest margin of victory in an ACC Men's Basketball Tournament semifinal. The win is the largest in an ACC semifinal since 2016, when North Carolina beat Notre Dame 78-47.

result.text: Miami March Madness opponent is Missouri in 2026 NCAA bracket Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Miami basketball’s March Madness opponent is Missouri. See the bracket Dan Rorabaugh USA TODAY NETWORK - Florida Show Caption Hide Caption Who’s ready to put on the glass slipper this March? Two underdog teams that could make a Cinderella run this March Madness are Saint Louis and Miami (Ohio). (This story has been updated to add new information about the other teams in Miami's March Madness bracket.) The Miami men's basketball team will play Missouri in the first round of the 2026 NCAA tournament . The Hurricanes (25-8) were awarded the No. 7 seed in the West Region. Jai Lucas ' first March Madness opponent as head coach will be the Tigers (20-12) out of the SEC. Miami completed a stunning turnaround in 2026 after going 7-24 last season. They returned to the postseason behind first-team All-ACC forward Malik Reneau and second-team guard Tre Donaldson, both Florida natives who transferred in from Indiana and Michigan, respectively. UM finished third in the ACC regular-season standings and reached the semifinals of the ACC tournament . Buy Miami NCAA tournament tickets The Hurricanes' biggest win of the season came Feb. 10 against North Carolina, when they beat Caleb Wilson and the Tar Heels 75-66 at the Watsco Center. They last appeared in the NCAA tournament in 2023, when they reached the Final Four as a 5-seed before losing to eventful champion UConn. Who does Miami play in 2026 NCAA tournament? Miami faces the Missouri Tigers in the first round. Mizzou went 20-12 and finished seventh in the SEC this season, though they were bounced by Kentucky in their SEC tournament opener in the quarterfinals. Missouri is in the big dance for the third time in four years under coach Dennis Gates. The Tigers boasted the No. 5 defense in their conference at 75.3 points per game and were 12th in offense at 79.7 ppg, though they were second in shooting 49% from the floor. Their best player is Mark Mitchell, a 6-foot-8 senior forward who made the All-SEC second team. He led the team in scoring with 18.3 ppg, good for sixth in the SEC, and in rebounding with 5.3 boards per game. Mizzou has two other players averaging double-digit points, Australian guard Jayden Stone and forward Trent Pierce. Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today!
